Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 229 upvotes, 18 comments
  2. Human Organ Atlas 57 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. Examples for the tcpdump and dig man pages 67 upvotes, 7 comments
  4. SBCL Fibers – Lightweight Cooperative Threads 134 upvotes, 26 comments
  5. Rack-mount hydroponics 286 upvotes, 69 comments
  6. Show HN: GDSL – 800 line kernel: Lisp subset in 500, C subset in 1300 17 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. Show HN: Signet – Autonomous wildfire tracking from satellite and weather data 76 upvotes, 19 comments
  8. Separating the Wayland Compositor and Window Manager 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  9. IBM, sonic delay lines, and the history of the 80×24 display (2019) 48 upvotes, 11 comments
  10. Learning athletic humanoid tennis skills from imperfect human motion data 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  11. Generating All 32-Bit Primes (Part I) 52 upvotes, 15 comments
  12. A most elegant TCP hole punching algorithm 174 upvotes, 69 comments
  13. Why Mathematica does not simplify sinh(arccosh(x)) 121 upvotes, 46 comments
  14. Zipp 2001 Restoration 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  15. Allow me to get to know you, mistakes and all 257 upvotes, 117 comments
  16. Show HN: What if your synthesizer was powered by APL (or a dumb K clone)? 44 upvotes, 14 comments
  17. Glassworm Is Back: A New Wave of Invisible Unicode Attacks Hits Repositories 72 upvotes, 27 comments
  18. Centuries of selective breeding turned wild cabbage into different vegetables 109 upvotes, 45 comments
  19. Kniterate Notes 29 upvotes, 8 comments
  20. Pentagon expands oversight of Stars and Stripes, limits content 135 upvotes, 59 comments
  21. The Webpage Has Instructions. The Agent Has Your Credentials 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  22. Show HN: Han – A Korean programming language written in Rust 199 upvotes, 107 comments
  23. UMD Scientists Create 'Smart Underwear' to Measure Human Flatulence 43 upvotes, 23 comments
  24. How kernel anti-cheats work 295 upvotes, 250 comments
  25. $96 3D-printed rocket that recalculates its mid-air trajectory using a $5 sensor 283 upvotes, 241 comments
  26. Codegen is not productivity 59 upvotes, 43 comments
  27. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 69 upvotes, 53 comments
  28. The 100 hour gap between a vibecoded prototype and a working product 144 upvotes, 180 comments
  29. Treasure hunter freed from jail after refusing to turn over shipwreck gold 151 upvotes, 201 comments
  30. Hollywood Enters Oscars Weekend in Existential Crisis 33 upvotes, 83 comments

Last generated 1:30am, 16 March 2026. Source here.
